name: Juneda - Arbeca - Torregrossa - Alamús - Juneda — gravel through the plains of Lérida
name_original: Juneda - Arbeca - Torregrossa - Alamús - Juneda — gravel por los llanos de Lérida
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Juneda are predominantly found within the plains of Lérida, characterized by a generally flat to gently rolling terrain. The region features significant natural elements like the Canal de Urgel, which is central to many routes. Surfaces are often paved, making them suitable for touring cyclists, though some routes incorporate gravel sections. This landscape provides consistent stretches for cycling and explores agricultural areas. Best touring cycling routes around Juneda The most popular touring cycling route is Juneda - Arbeca - Torregrossa - Alamús - Juneda — gravel through the plains of Lérida, a 34.6 miles (55.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route explores the expansive plains of Lérida, featuring both paved and gravel surfaces. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Juneda - Borges via the Canal de Urgel — Circular route through Les Garrigues, a moderate 18.3 miles (29.5 km) path. It follows the Canal de Urgel, offering a circular journey through the Les Garrigues region. Local touring cyclists also love the Salt del Duran Waterfall – Third Canal of Urgell Path loop from Juneda, a 24.6 miles (39.6 km) trail leading through the Third Canal of Urgell Path and past the Salt del Duran Waterfall, often completed in about 3 hours 18 minutes. Touring cycling around Juneda is defined by the Canal de Urgel, the plains of Lérida, and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to moderate challenges. The routes in Juneda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 140 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Juneda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Juneda?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes around Juneda, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. You can find routes ranging from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Juneda?
A: The touring cycling routes around Juneda are predominantly found within the plains of Lérida, characterized by a generally flat to gently rolling terrain. Many routes feature significant natural elements like the Canal de Urgel. Surfaces are often paved, making them suitable for touring cyclists, though some routes incorporate gravel sections, exploring agricultural areas.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Juneda?
A: Yes, Juneda offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20153067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Banqueta de Juneda – Nou Salts of the Canals d'Urgell loop from Juneda&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 12.5-mile (20.1 km) route with minimal elevation gain, ideal for a relaxed ride.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Juneda?
A: The touring cycling routes in Juneda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 140 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Juneda's varied terrain, often praising the scenic routes along the Canal de Urgel and the peaceful agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Juneda?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Juneda are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962317559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Juneda - Borges via the Canal de Urgel — Circular route through Les Garrigues&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 18.3-mile (29.5 km) path that follows the Canal de Urgel.

Q: Can I find touring cycling routes that follow the Canal de Urgel?
A: The Canal de Urgel is a central feature of many touring cycling routes in the Juneda region. You can enjoy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10470786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Path Near Lleida – Tòfol Footbridge loop from Juneda&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 35.7-mile (57.5 km) journey along the canal.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can cycle to natural attractions like waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20156232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Duran Waterfall – Third Canal of Urgell Path loop from Juneda&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 24.6-mile (39.6 km) trail that leads past the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Duran Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while cycling around Juneda?
A: While cycling around Juneda, you can discover several natural attractions and landmarks. Besides the prominent Canal de Urgel, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Duran Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3776964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Second Machine&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features green spaces like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Park&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5175602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque del Terrall&lt;/a&gt;, as well as various irrigation ponds and reservoirs such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassella Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Juneda?
A: The plains of Lérida, where Juneda is located, generally offer pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be quite warm, while winter might bring cooler temperatures, but many routes remain accessible due to the predominantly paved surfaces.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders in Juneda?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, Juneda offers several difficult touring cycling routes. These routes often feature longer distances and more elevation gain, sometimes incorporating unpaved segments. There are 7 difficult routes available for those looking for a more strenuous ride.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that offer a mix of paved and gravel surfaces?
A: Absolutely. Some routes in Juneda provide a varied cycling experience with both paved and gravel sections. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968569908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Juneda - Arbeca - Torregrossa - Alamús - Juneda — gravel through the plains of Lérida&lt;/a&gt;, a 34.6-mile (55.7 km) route that explores the expansive plains with mixed surfaces.

Q: Can I find routes that are less crowded for a peaceful touring cycling experience?
A: While specific crowd levels can vary, many routes exploring the agricultural plains and the Canal de Urgel offer a peaceful experience away from urban centers. Opting for routes during off-peak hours or weekdays can further enhance the tranquility of your ride through the Lérida countryside.

Church of Sant Martí in the town of Alamús, in the province of Lleida. It was built in the 16th century on top of the old mosque and is Romanesque and Baroque in style.
